briefs.title: Vancouver Council urges senior governments to fund housing, shelters, and income supports
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council reviewed the 2025 Homeless Count results and formally asked the Provincial and Federal governments to act. The requests include higher income supports, a new navigation centre (a hub that connects people to services), expanded treatment programs, better discharge planning (helping people leaving hospitals or jails find stable housing), and more daytime shelter and supported housing options.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Healthcare, Professional Services

briefs.title: Vancouver Council approves $9.4M+ in cultural grants across four funding streams
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved more than $9.4 million in cultural grants covering institutional, annual, and three-year operating support, plus capital grants (one-time funding for physical projects or buildings) of $250,000 to the Vancouver Concert Hall and Theatre Society and $728,000 to Historic Joy Kogawa House Society.
briefs.action_required: Check Vancouver's cultural grant streams to see if your arts or cultural organization qualifies to apply.
briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail

briefs.title: Vancouver approves $89,000 in community sport event grants for 19 organizations in Spring 2026
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved 19 Community Sport Event Grants totalling $89,000 from the 2026 Vancouver Community Sport Hosting Program Budget. These grants support organizations hosting community sport events in Vancouver.
briefs.action_required: Check if your organization qualifies for future Vancouver Community Sport Event Grant rounds and apply early.
briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail

briefs.title: 2025 Property Tax Exemptions Report Received by Council — No Changes Made
briefs.summary: Local council reviewed a report on 2025 property tax exemptions (reductions or waivers on the amount of property tax owed) and accepted it for information only. No new decisions, changes, or actions were taken — the report was simply noted.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services

briefs.title: Vancouver adopts Coastal Flood Management Policy — new rules for waterfront planning and building
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has officially adopted a Coastal Flood Management Policy. This policy sets out how the City will plan for and respond to flooding along its coastline, using both physical barriers (like seawalls) and non-physical tools (like zoning rules and land-use planning) to reduce flood risk.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or development plans are near Vancouver's coastline and review how this policy may affect future permits or zoning.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Vancouver Heritage Foundation receives $152,375 city grant for 2026–2028 operating period
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council approved a $152,375 operating grant to the Vancouver Heritage Foundation for the first year of its 2026–2028 funding agreement. The Foundation supports the preservation of heritage buildings and neighbourhoods across Vancouver.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Alcohol now permitted in designated public plazas under new city by-law through 2028
briefs.summary: The city has updated its land use rules to allow people to drink alcohol in specific public plazas as part of the 2026–2029 Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program. This change was approved in principle by Council and applies only to designated (officially named and approved) public spaces.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is near a designated plaza — new foot traffic and alcohol rules may affect your operations.

key_dates.label: Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program — by-law takes effect through 2028

briefs.industries: Liquor, Food Service, Retail

briefs.title: Vancouver applies for $200,000 to speed up housing development approvals
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved applying for $200,000 in provincial funding to redesign how the city processes housing development applications. If the funding is granted, the project aims to make approvals faster and more predictable for developers and builders.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Vancouver Housing Service Redesign project for changes to development approval timelines and processes.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Housing Agreement By-laws updated to amend rezoning conditions across multiple developments
briefs.summary: City council approved changes to the rezoning conditions (the rules attached to a property when its land use is changed) for several housing developments. New Housing Agreement By-laws — legal documents that lock in specific requirements like affordability or rental terms — will be drafted to reflect these updates.
briefs.action_required: Check with your city planner if your development is affected and review any updated Housing Agreement By-law terms.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: 5590 Victoria Drive rezoned: affordable rental units converted to market-rate rental housing
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council approved a zoning change for 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue. The building's rental units, which were previously locked in as affordable non-profit housing, will now be allowed to rent at market rates (whatever the open market will bear, with no price restrictions). The floor space ratio (a measure of how much total floor area a building can have relative to its lot size) was also slightly increased from 3.49 to 3.5.
briefs.action_required: If you own or operate rental property near this site, review how nearby market-rate units may affect your rents and tenant demand.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Tenant

briefs.title: 4088 Granville Street rezoned for 31-unit rental development — density more than doubled
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved in principle a rezoning (a change to the official land-use rules) for 4088 Granville Street. The site will shift from its current zoning to CD-1 (a custom zoning category tailored to a specific project), allowing three three-storey rental buildings with 31 units. The allowable floor space ratio (a measure of how much building you can put on a lot) rises from 0.5 to 1.2.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near 4088 Granville St, review how this new development may affect your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: 82-unit mixed-use rental building approved for West 10th Avenue in Vancouver
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council approved a rezoning (a change to the rules about how land can be used) for properties at 3803–3823 West 10th Avenue and 2553 Highbury Street. The land will change from a residential zone to a custom development zone, allowing a six-storey building with 82 secured market rental units, including some below-market units if not built within two years.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease nearby property, review how this new development may affect your business operations or lease.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: 310 East 14th Avenue rezoned for 20-storey rental tower with 20% below-market units
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved in principle a rezoning of 310 East 14th Avenue, changing its land-use designation (the rules about what can be built there) from low-density residential to a custom high-density zone. This allows a 20-storey rental building — roughly 60 metres tall — where 20% of units must be rented at below-market rates.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property nearby, assess how increased density and new rental supply may affect your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: West 13th Avenue rezoned to higher-density residential, enabling larger apartment buildings
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council approved a rezoning of 1305–1325 West 13th Avenue from R3-3 to R5-4 Residential District. This means the land can now support larger, higher-density residential buildings than before, and a Housing Agreement By-law (a legal contract tied to the property that sets rules on how housing must be used) was approved alongside it.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property nearby, review how increased density may affect your business or property plans.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Amenity Cost Charge By-law and Density Bonus Rules Open for Public Consultation
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has directed staff to gather public and industry feedback on two proposed changes: a new Amenity Cost Charge By-law (a fee developers pay to fund community amenities like parks and rec centres) and updates to density bonus rules (which allow builders to add extra floors or units in exchange for community benefits) under Bill 16.
briefs.action_required: Submit feedback during the upcoming public consultation if these charges or zoning changes affect your development plans.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Vancouver property owners near Trounce Alley and Blood Alley to be charged 2025 maintenance costs
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14670, which charges nearby property owners for the 2025 upkeep costs of Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square. This is a 'local improvement assessment' (a special charge added to your property tax bill to cover shared infrastructure costs in your area).
briefs.action_required: Check if your property is in the Trounce Alley or Blood Alley area and review your 2025 property tax bill for the new charge.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services

briefs.title: East Hastings Street Collective Parking Project 2025 costs charged to nearby properties via new by-law
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14669, which places a special charge (called a 'real property assessment') on properties near East Hastings Street to help pay for the 2025 costs of the Collective Parking Project. This means some property owners in that area may see an added cost on their property tax bill tied to this project.
briefs.action_required: Check if your East Hastings area property is subject to By-law No. 14669 and review your tax bill for new charges.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Housing Agreement locked in for 320–360 West 2nd Avenue under Vancouver By-law 14668
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14668, which creates a legally binding Housing Agreement for the property at 320–360 West 2nd Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a registered contract on a property's title that sets rules about how the units must be used — for example, keeping them as rental housing — and those rules stay with the property even if it is sold.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Housing Agreement amended for three Vancouver properties on Prior Street, Malkin Avenue, and Station Street
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14667, which amends the Housing Agreement (a legal contract registered on a property's title that sets rules about how housing units must be used or rented) covering three addresses: 450–496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue, and 1002 Station Street. The specific changes to the agreement's terms have not been detailed in the public notice.
briefs.action_required: If you own or manage property at these three addresses, review the amended Housing Agreement terms with your lawyer.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Zoning rules updated for 1700–1750 West 75th Avenue in Vancouver
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14666, which updates the zoning rules (the local laws that control how land and buildings can be used) for the property at 1700–1750 West 75th Avenue. This change amends the existing CD-1 (198) by-law, meaning the permitted uses or development conditions for that specific site have been modified.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop property at 1700–1750 West 75th Avenue, review the updated by-law with your lawyer or planner.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: 1710–1730 East Pender Street rezoned to CD-1 by Vancouver City Council
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council passed By-law No. 14665, which rezones the property at 1710–1730 East Pender Street to CD-1 (a site-specific zoning designation that sets custom rules for what can be built and how the land can be used). This change affects what types of development and business uses are now permitted at that address.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to operate at 1710–1730 East Pender Street, review the new CD-1 zoning rules with your lawyer or planner.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 6486 Chester Street rezoned to CD-1, opening site for new development
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has passed By-law No. 14664, which rezones the property at 6486 Chester Street to CD-1 (a 'Comprehensive Development' zone — meaning the site can now be developed under a custom set of rules specific to that property). This change affects what can be built or operated at that address.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop 6486 Chester Street, review the new CD-1 zoning rules with a planner.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: East 12th Avenue site rezoned to CD-1, opening door to new development
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has passed By-law No. 14663, rezoning the property at 1302–1318 East 12th Avenue to CD-1 (a custom zoning category that sets specific rules for what can be built on a single site). This means the land can now be developed under those new rules, which may affect nearby property owners, tenants, and businesses.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near 1302–1318 East 12th Avenue, review the CD-1 zoning rules to understand how nearby development may affect you.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: 29-Storey Mixed-Use Tower at Cambie & Smithe Heads to Public Hearing — 165 Rental Units, 246 Hotel Rooms
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has sent a rezoning application (a request to change how land can be used) for 888–896 Cambie Street and 111–191 Smithe Street to a Public Hearing. The proposal would allow a 29-storey building with 165 rental apartments and 246 hotel rooms, and would protect the facade (exterior front) of the historic Stanley Brock Building.
briefs.action_required: Attend or submit written comments at the upcoming Public Hearing if this development affects your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Vancouver approves in principle 16-storey, 152-room hotel rezoning at 888 West 8th Avenue
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved in principle a rezoning (a change to the rules about how land can be used) at 888 West 8th Avenue. The site will shift from a general commercial zone to a custom development zone, allowing a 16-storey hotel with 152 rooms and a much higher building density (FSR — the ratio of total floor space to lot size — rising from 3.0 to 8.1).
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease nearby property or a competing hotel, monitor City of Vancouver rezoning updates for the final approval hearing.
briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Vancouver creates C-2A zoning district to speed up rental housing and small hotel approvals
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council has approved a new zoning category called C-2A (a mixed-use commercial district that allows both businesses and housing in the same building) to make it faster and easier to build rental apartments and small hotels. Several city bylaws — including rules on zoning, parking, signage, and noise — are being updated to support this new district.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or planned development falls within a C-2A zone — it may open new building or permit options.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: 2026 property tax split set at 57.5% residential, 42.5% non-residential — rates to follow
briefs.summary: Council has decided how the 2026 property tax burden will be shared: residential properties will cover 57.5% of the total, and non-residential properties (such as commercial and industrial) will cover 42.5%. The Director of Finance has been directed to calculate the actual tax rates that achieve this split.
briefs.action_required: Watch for your 2026 property tax rate notice and budget for a possible change in your tax bill.

key_dates.label: 2026 non-residential property tax rates take effect

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Construction & Development

briefs.title: City approves $2.39B budget with $1.22B property tax levy for 2026
briefs.summary: City council has approved its 2026 budget, setting total spending at $2.39 billion and a General Purposes Tax Levy (the portion of city costs funded directly by property taxes) of $1.22 billion. This means property owners and businesses that pay property tax can expect their bills to reflect this levy in 2026.
briefs.action_required: Review your 2026 property tax bill when it arrives to confirm the new levy amount is correct.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Construction & Development
